question: I work from home as a W-2 employee in the US. Can I deduct my home-office expenses on my 2024 federal return?
panel_summary: All four live answers say W-2 employees generally cannot deduct home-office expenses for 2024 because the employee deduction was suspended through 2025, citing IRS pages; markers resolve. Whether this correctly applies to the asker's facts requires a qualified human reviewer.
label: faithful
notes: D5 tax/financial case — SUBJECT-MATTER CORRECTNESS IS DEFERRED. The engine's structural signals are asserted (grounded, not a refusal, no high-stakes safety caveat), but whether the tax conclusion is correct for a given taxpayer, and whether it is still current, is a professional judgment recorded in the operator queue. Tax law is jurisdiction- and year-specific; the fixture never asserts the answer is right.

initial_answers.answer_text:
## Short answer

For a W-2 employee, generally no for 2024. The Tax Cuts and Jobs Act suspended the miscellaneous itemized deduction for unreimbursed employee expenses, including the employee home-office deduction, for tax years 2018 through 2025 [1]. The home-office deduction remains available to self-employed filers on Schedule C, but not to employees who receive a W-2 [2]. If your employer has an accountable reimbursement plan, that is the usual route.

sources.title: IRS Topic No. 509, Business Use of Home

initial_answers.answer_text:
### The rule

Employees paid on a W-2 cannot take the federal home-office deduction for 2024. The deduction for unreimbursed employee business expenses was eliminated through 2025 by the 2017 tax law, so it is not available on your federal return [1]. It applies to the self-employed and certain business owners, reported with the business income [2]. Some states still allow an employee version; the federal answer is no.

sources.title: IRS Topic No. 509, Business Use of Home

initial_answers.answer_text:
## Position

On the federal return, a W-2 employee generally may not deduct home-office costs for 2024. The suspension of miscellaneous itemized deductions under current law runs through 2025 and covers the employee home-office deduction [1]. The deduction is preserved for self-employed taxpayers who use part of the home regularly and exclusively for business [2]. Check whether your state diverges, because several do.

sources.title: IRS Topic No. 509, Business Use of Home

initial_answers.answer_text: **Summary.** No for a W-2 employee on the 2024 federal return. The unreimbursed-employee-expense deduction, which is where an employee home office would have gone, is suspended through 2025 [1]. Self-employed filers still qualify under the regular-and-exclusive-use test [2]. If you have both W-2 and self-employed income, only the self-employed portion could support a home-office claim.

sources.title: IRS Topic No. 509, Business Use of Home

final_synthesis.consensus: All four models agree that a W-2 employee generally cannot deduct home-office expenses on the 2024 federal return, because the unreimbursed-employee-expense deduction is suspended through 2025; the deduction remains available to self-employed filers [1] [2].
final_synthesis.disagreement: No material disagreement. The models add different side notes about state treatment and mixed W-2 / self-employed income.
final_synthesis.source_support: The answers cite IRS home-office and business-use-of-home pages, and their inline markers resolve to those sources.
final_synthesis.uncertainty: The panel does not know your state, whether you have any self-employed income, or your employer's reimbursement arrangements, all of which can change the outcome.
final_synthesis.recommendation: Treat this as general information, not tax advice for your situation. Confirm with a tax professional or the current IRS instructions before filing.
